name: IBM 2026 Measurement Framework
north_star_metric: Marketing-sourced qualified pipeline for the software and AI portfolios

metrics.name: watsonx consideration / shortlist rate
metrics.definition: Share of tracked enterprise AI evaluations in which watsonx is shortlisted against hyperscaler alternatives
metrics.supports_objective: Drive watsonx consideration among enterprise AI buyers evaluating hyperscaler alternatives
metrics.target: Up year-over-year; closing the gap to hyperscaler shortlist rates

metrics.name: Red Hat-attached hybrid cloud pipeline
metrics.definition: Marketing-sourced qualified pipeline on modernization deals that attach Red Hat / OpenShift
metrics.supports_objective: Grow Red Hat adoption within hybrid cloud modernization deals

metrics.name: AI/automation consulting qualified pipeline
metrics.definition: Marketing-sourced qualified pipeline for IBM Consulting engagements tied to AI and automation transformation
metrics.supports_objective: Expand IBM Consulting pipeline tied to AI and automation transformation engagements

metrics.name: Brand relevance among next-gen enterprise buyers
metrics.definition: Brand-tracker relevance and consideration scores among younger enterprise technology decision-makers
metrics.supports_objective: Strengthen brand relevance with a younger generation of enterprise technology buyers

metrics.name: Enterprise-trust perception
metrics.definition: Brand-tracker trust and credibility scores among enterprise buyers — must not erode as AI messaging scales
metrics.supports_objective: Maintain enterprise-grade trust while scaling AI messaging
metrics.target: Hold or improve vs. prior year

measurement_cadence: Monthly pipeline review; quarterly brand and strategic review
measurement_approach: Marketing attribution on CRM pipeline for demand metrics; quarterly brand tracker for consideration, relevance, and trust. Directional targets pending internal planning.
source: Built from public information: IBM 2023 Annual Report, Q4 2023 earnings commentary, and ibm.com (2024). Metrics and targets inferred from public strategic priorities; not internal scorecard values.
